authoritative_sources.title: Franchise Rule
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC is the primary federal authority for franchise disclosure obligations, franchise terminology, and baseline disclosure records that govern franchise award and onboarding workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: Franchise Rule Compliance Guide
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC compliance guidance explains how Item 11 assistance and training disclosures, Item 23 receipt handling, and agreement timing shape operational onboarding prerequisites.

authoritative_sources.title: Statement of Policy Regarding Franchise Registration and Disclosure Guidelines
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASAA coordinates state franchise registration administrators, and its public policy statement is the recognized multistate operating baseline for registration filings, state addenda, receipt treatment, and disclosure consistency.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: State registration, addenda, and filing-readiness review

authoritative_sources.title: Electronic Franchise and Business Opportunity Depository
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EFD is the public filing portal for participating franchise registration states and therefore an authoritative reference for one of the industry systems of record that can block onboarding progression.

authoritative_sources.title: SBA Franchise Directory
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SBA is authoritative for SBA lending program eligibility, and the public Franchise Directory is a direct workflow reference when financing or lender-readiness prerequisites affect award-to-opening onboarding.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5, Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 is a recognized public control standard for account management, access approvals, and training-related controls, which directly constrains onboarding access, enablement, and auditability decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-63B, Digital Identity Guidelines: Authentication and Lifecycle Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST digital identity guidance is a recognized public workflow reference for authenticator lifecycle and secure enrollment, making it directly relevant to franchise portal and admin-account readiness decisions.

workflow_stages.name: Franchise disclosure and award intake validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the onboarding request is tied to a valid franchise award package with the correct FDD, agreement, receipt trail, state addenda, and jurisdiction context before downstream setup begins.

workflow_stages.name: Setup, training, and permission readiness
workflow_stages.description: Validate Item 11 training obligations, operations-manual acknowledgment, IAM approvals, MFA, role grants, and onboarding checklist sequencing before enablement is treated as complete.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Systems, vendor, and financing dependency review
workflow_stages.description: Check franchise CRM, LMS, IAM, approved-vendor, royalty or ACH, and financing-related dependencies so opening work does not move forward on stale or partial setup evidence.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Readiness confirmation and go-live handoff
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether remaining blockers, approvals, and deferred items allow a controlled handoff to steady-state operations without implying legal or operating signoff.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Authority conflict and specialized-lane escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when legal interpretation, state registration sufficiency, financing approval, permit readiness, or broader operating authority changes the safe answer.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Franchise Disclosure Document receipt and revision trail
Signed franchise agreement package and state addenda
Item 11 training plan and LMS completion records
Operations-manual acknowledgment and opening checklist milestones
IAM tickets, role grants, MFA enrollment evidence, and portal provisioning records
Approved-vendor setup records, royalty or ACH setup work items, and finance or lender dependency notes
Readiness summaries, escalation packets, and go-live handoff notes
systems_of_record: State registration tracking or EFD reference workflow
Identity and access management or SSO platform
Unit opening checklist or project-tracking system
Finance, royalty, or payment setup system

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Validate franchise-specific onboarding artifacts, setup evidence, permissions, training, and dependency status.
Produce onboarding checklists, readiness summaries, go-live handoff notes, and escalation packets without overstating authority.
Route work to legal, finance, identity, field operations, or permitting owners when ownership changes.
escalate: Required FDD receipt, franchise agreement, state addenda, training, IAM approval, or opening evidence is missing or materially conflicts.
The request requires legal interpretation, state registration sufficiency judgment, financing approval, vendor exception approval, or permit signoff.
Tenant policy and public source guidance materially conflict on disclosure timing, access posture, or go-live readiness.
The public baseline is narrowed by jurisdiction, franchise brand controls, or tenant operating rules that are missing at runtime.
refuse: Mark onboarding complete or go-live ready without evidence-backed prerequisite closure.
Treat franchise onboarding as generic customer or partner onboarding without franchise-specific records and controls.
Approve legal, financial, permit, or contractual exceptions outside the lane.

domain_failure_modes: A signed franchise agreement is treated as sufficient even though Item 11 training, access approvals, or opening checklist evidence is incomplete.
State addenda, FDD receipt, or EFD-related filing posture is ignored and the lane overstates readiness.
Portal or admin access is granted with broader permissions than approved because onboarding focuses on speed instead of control evidence.
Financing, royalty, or vendor dependencies are treated as cosmetic and not as go-live blockers when they materially affect opening readiness.
The lane drifts into legal interpretation, territory exceptions, or registration sufficiency instead of escalating to specialized owners.
The industry label is treated as cosmetic and the pack collapses into generic onboarding language without FDD, Item 11, Item 23, addenda, or opening-checklist artifacts.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Setup blocked by missing credentials, permissions, or training
Ambiguous ownership between onboarding and steady-state support

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-by-state franchise registration, exemption, addenda, and local permit obligations vary materially, and brand-specific opening controls can be stricter than the public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack records the public FTC, NASAA, SBA, and NIST baseline but requires tenant-supplied jurisdiction, brand policy, opening-control, and owner-routing context before final readiness decisions are made.

notes: The research baseline covers franchise workflow, evidence, terminology, systems of record, and refusal conditions rather than generic onboarding mechanics.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging because state and tenant differences are explicitly handled through retrieval and escalation.
The lane stops at readiness validation and handoff packaging. Legal interpretation, financing approval, permit sufficiency, and steady-state operating ownership remain outside the lane.
